func solve(points [][]int64) int64 {
    const MOD = 1000000007
    inv2 := 500000004 // Modular inverse of 2 modulo 1e9+7

    yCount := make(map[int64]int64)
    for _, p := range points {
        y := p[1]
        yCount[y]++
    }

    sumA := int64(0)
    sumASq := int64(0)

    for y, cnt := range yCount {
        temp := cnt * (cnt - 1)
        a_i := (temp * inv2) % MOD
        sumA = (sumA + a_i) % MOD
        sumASq = (sumASq + a_i*a_i) % MOD
    }

    temp := (sumA*sumA - sumASq + MOD) % MOD
    ans := (temp * inv2) % MOD
    return ans
}
```

But the code must handle the case where the same y appears multiple times. The code groups them with a Map.

So the code is:

function solve(points: number[][]): number {

    const MOD = 10**9 +7;

    const inv2 = 500000004;

    const yMap = new Map<number, number>();

    for (const [x, y] of points) {

        const count = yMap.get(y) || 0;

        yMap.set(y, count + 1);

    }

    let sum_a = 0;

    let sum_a_sq = 0;

    for (const [y, cnt] of yMap.entries()) {

        const temp = cnt * (cnt - 1);

        const a_i = (temp * inv2) % MOD;

        sum_a = (sum_a + a_i) % MOD;

        sum_a_sq = (sum_a_sq + a_i * a_i) % MOD;

    }

    const temp_val = (sum_a * sum_a - sum_a_sq + MOD) % MOD;

    const ans = (temp_val * inv2) % MOD;

    return ans;

}
